Many other Numpy functions support the `axis` keyword argument to specify the axis along which the respective operation should be applied, it would be great to have such an argument for this function as well.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/11583/reactions","total_count":2,"+1":2,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/11583/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"completed"},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2429543713","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/11583#issuecomment-2429543713","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/11583","id":2429543713,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAA3dP86Qz-Uh","user":{"login":"dasiff","id":126210147,"node_id":"U_kgDOB4XQYw","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/126210147?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/dasiff","html_url":"https://github.com/dasiff","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dasiff/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dasiff/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dasiff/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dasiff/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dasiff/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dasiff/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dasiff/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dasiff/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dasiff/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-10-22T15:05:59Z","updated_at":"2024-10-22T15:05:59Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Actually the proposed code does not do what a single-dimensional shuffle should do:
a = np.arange(40).reshape((5,8))       
print(str(a))
# shuffle along axis 1     
np.apply_along_axis(np.random.shuffle,1,a)
print(str(a)) 

[[ 0  5  3  7  6  1  2  4]
 [11  9  8 15 13 12 14 10]
 [18 23 19 20 21 16 22 17]
 [28 29 24 25 26 31 27 30]
 [39 38 34 33 37 32 36 35]]

It shuffles each row separately--it should preserve the column order and shuffle columns ","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2429543713/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
